_comment: Doll (in-game: Collection Item) LEVELING ECONOMY. Owner-provided 2026-07-15 (owner-stated values = authoritative; a couple of semantics flagged TBD below). Super-success PROC CHANCES live in data/doll-super-success.json. Dolls are R or SR rarity, phases 1-15. This is DELIVERABLE 2 (doll leveling optimizer) data — PARKED while OL rolling (deliverable 1) is built. This project currently models only the SR-phase-15 stat endpoint (docs/data/gear-doll.md).
_toolboxNote: Maintenance-kit ('toolbox') EXP by tier. Confirms the previously-INFERRED Blue/Purple/Gold = R/SR/SSR = 200/500/1000 mapping — now owner-CONFIRMED.
_xpPerLevelNote: XP to advance one phase, by DOLL rarity. R doll = 1000 xp/level, SR doll = 3000 xp/level.

superSuccess.effect: On a super-success proc, jump forward to the next checkpoint (5 / 10 / 15) with accumulated XP reset to 0.
superSuccess.example: level 3 holding 200 xp + super-success proc -> level 5 with 0 xp (owner).
superSuccess.procChanceSource: data/doll-super-success.json (percent, by collectible-rarity x toolbox-tier x phase step)
superSuccess._openQuestion: Exact jump target per step (a proc from level 4 -> 5 vs overshoot to 10?) and whether the proc rolls per toolbox-feed or per level-up. Pin before modeling.

rarityUpgrade.path: A maxed R doll (phase 15) upgrades to an SR doll at phase 5 (XP carries: R15 -> SR5).
rarityUpgrade.note: Community-standard path. Raw-SR leveling from phase 1 is considered a loss vs laundering through a cheap R doll first.

tradeIn.decision: The optimizer must weigh (A) level an R doll to phase 15 then upgrade -> SR phase 5, vs (B) trade 4 R dolls for the chance table above and level an SR doll (from phase 0/5) with the won materials. Compare expected total toolbox cost + doll inventory to reach SR phase 15.

kitDropRates._comment: Kit ('toolbox') box drop rates, owner-provided 2026-07-15. Derived ONLY from the box that yields all three tiers (owner: closest to a year of observed drops); the R-only/SR box is intentionally excluded. Used to DERIVE the scarcity usage-weight of each kit tier = 1 / (expected kits of that tier per box-open). This is the optimizer's COST objective, distinct from toolboxExp (the EXP a kit adds when fed). Supply per open: R 3.5 / SR 0.4 / SSR 0.2 -> weights R 0.2857 / SR 2.5 / SSR 5.0 opens-per-kit (relative 1 : 8.75 : 17.5).
